name: Seitan
recipeIngredient: 6 cups Stone ground, whole wheat bread flour
12 Slices fresh ginger, about 1/8 inch thick
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix together the flour and water to make a dough. Knead until stiff (10 to 12 minutes by hand or 6 to 8 minutes by machine).
2. Place in a bowl, cover with cold water, and allow to rest for about 10 minutes.
3. While the dough is resting, bring 3 quarts of water to a boil. Add tamari, ginger, and kombu. Cook for 15 minutes, then remove from heat and allow to cool completely.
4. Immerse the dough in a bowl of warm water and knead to remove starch. When the water turns milky, drain it off and refill the bowl with fresh water. In final rinses, use cold water to tighten gluten. Rinsing is complete when liquid runs clear as the dough is squeezed (about 8 changes of water).
5. Lightly oil a 9-inch x 5-inch x 3-inch loaf pan. Place seitan in the pan and let rest until the dough relaxes, about 10 minutes.
6. Use in recipes calling for uncooked seitan or continue with following steps for cooked seitan:
a. Bring 3 quarts of plain water to a boil. Add seitan and cook for 30 to 45 minutes or until it floats to the surface. Drain and cut into pieces or leave whole.
b. Place seitan in cold tamari stock and bring to a boil. Lower temperature and simmer for 1 to 1.5 hours, depending on size of pieces.
7. Serve warm or use in recipe calling for cooked seitan.
